final_output:
# Task

Plan a multi-tenant SaaS application with these requirements:

1. **Core Feature**: Users upload documents (PDF, DOCX), the system extracts text, generates summaries, answers questions about the documents using RAG.

2. **Multi-tenancy**: Each tenant has isolated data. Tenants can have multiple users with role-based access (admin, editor, viewer).

3. **Scale Target**: 100 tenants, 1000 users, 10,000 documents per tenant.

4. **Tech Constraints**: Python backend, React frontend, PostgreSQL database, must run on AWS.

5. **Timeline**: MVP in 8 weeks, full product in 16 weeks.

6. **Budget**: Small team (2 backend, 1 frontend, 1 DevOps).

Produce a comprehensive plan that a development team could execute.

---

# Working Document

## 1. Architecture Overview

### 1.1 High-Level System Diagram

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        React Frontend                           │
│              (S3 + CloudFront static hosting)                   │
└──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘
                           │ HTTPS (REST + WebSocket for status)
                           ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    AWS Application Load Balancer                │
└──────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘
                           ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              API Layer (FastAPI on ECS Fargate)                  │
│   ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌────────────────────┐       │
│   │ Auth &   │  │  Document    │  │   RAG / Q&A        │       │
│   │ Tenancy  │  │  Management  │  │   Service           │       │
│   └──────────┘  └──────────────┘  └────────────────────┘       │
└──────┬──────────────┬───────────────────┬───────────────────────┘
       │              │                   │
       ▼              ▼                   ▼
┌────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐
│ PostgreSQL │ │  S3 Bucket  │  │  Vector Store        │
│  (RDS)     │ │ (Documents) │  │  (pgvector on RDS)   │
└────────────┘ └─────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘
                      │
                      ▼
       ┌──────────────────────────┐
       │  Async Worker (Celery    │
       │  on ECS Fargate)         │
       │  - Text extraction       │
       │  - Embedding generation  │
       │  - Summary generation    │
       └────────────┬─────────────┘
                    │
                    ▼
       ┌──────────────────────────┐
       │  SQS Queue / Redis       │
       │  (Task broker)           │
       └──────────────────────────┘
```

### 1.2 Key Architectural Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| API Framework | FastAPI | Async support, auto-docs, Pydantic validation, Python ecosystem |
| Multi-tenancy model | Shared DB, shared schema with `tenant_id` column + RLS | Cost-effective at 100 tenants; PostgreSQL Row-Level Security enforces isolation |
| Vector store | pgvector extension on same RDS | Reduces infra complexity; sufficient at our scale (~1M vectors per tenant) |
| Async processing | Celery + SQS (or Redis) | Document processing is CPU/IO-heavy; must be async |
| LLM provider | OpenAI API (GPT-4o / embeddings) | Fastest to MVP; can swap to Bedrock later |
| Container orchestration | ECS Fargate | Serverless containers; simpler than EKS for small team |
| Auth | AWS Cognito + custom RBAC | Managed auth reduces build time; custom RBAC layer for tenant roles |

---

## 2. Data Model

### 2.1 Core Database Schema

```sql
-- Enable required extensions
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "uuid-ossp";
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "pgvector";

-- ============================================================
-- TENANTS
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE tenants (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    slug VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    plan VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'free',          -- free, pro, enterprise
    settings JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
    max_documents INT DEFAULT 10000,
    max_users INT DEFAULT 50,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- ============================================================
-- USERS
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE users (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    tenant_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    full_name VARCHAR(255),
    cognito_sub VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE,          -- Links to Cognito user
    role VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'viewer', -- admin, editor, viewer
    is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    UNIQUE(tenant_id, email)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_users_tenant ON users(tenant_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_users_cognito ON users(cognito_sub);

-- ============================================================
-- DOCUMENTS
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE documents (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    tenant_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    uploaded_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    title VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
    file_name VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
    file_type VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,           -- pdf, docx
    file_size_bytes BIGINT,
    s3_key VARCHAR(1000) NOT NULL,
    status VARCHAR(30) DEFAULT 'uploaded',    -- uploaded, processing, ready, failed
    processing_error TEXT,
    extracted_text TEXT,                       -- full extracted text (for reference)
    summary TEXT,                             -- AI-generated summary
    page_count INT,
    metadata JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_documents_tenant ON documents(tenant_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_documents_status ON documents(tenant_id, status);
CREATE INDEX idx_documents_uploaded_by ON documents(uploaded_by);

-- ============================================================
-- DOCUMENT CHUNKS (for RAG)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE document_chunks (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    tenant_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    document_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    chunk_index INT NOT NULL,
    content TEXT NOT NULL,
    token_count INT,
    embedding vector(1536),                   -- OpenAI ada-002 / text-embedding-3-small
    metadata JSONB DEFAULT '{}',              -- page_number, section, etc.
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_chunks_document ON document_chunks(document_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_chunks_tenant ON document_chunks(tenant_id);

-- HNSW index for vector similarity search (per-tenant filtering)
CREATE INDEX idx_chunks_embedding ON document_chunks
    USING hnsw (embedding vector_cosine_ops)
    WITH (m = 16, ef_construction = 64);

-- ============================================================
-- CONVERSATIONS (Q&A sessions)
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE conversations (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    tenant_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    title VARCHAR(500),
    document_ids UUID[] DEFAULT '{}',         -- scoped to specific documents (optional)
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_conversations_tenant_user ON conversations(tenant_id, user_id);

CREATE TABLE messages (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    tenant_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    conversation_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES conversations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    role VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,                -- user, assistant
    content TEXT NOT NULL,
    source_chunks UUID[] DEFAULT '{}',        -- references to document_chunks used
    token_usage JSONB DEFAULT '{}',           -- prompt_tokens, completion_tokens
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_messages_conversation ON messages(conversation_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_messages_tenant ON messages(tenant_id);

-- ============================================================
-- AUDIT LOG
-- ============================================================
CREATE TABLE audit_logs (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    tenant_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    user_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    action VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,             -- document.upload, document.delete, user.invite, etc.
    resource_type VARCHAR(50),
    resource_id UUID,
    details JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
    ip_address INET,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_audit_tenant_time ON audit_logs(tenant_id, created_at DESC);

-- ============================================================
-- ROW-LEVEL SECURITY
-- ============================================================
ALTER TABLE documents ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE document_chunks ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE conversations ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE messages ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE users ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE audit_logs ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- Example RLS policy (applied via SET app.current_tenant_id per request)
CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation_documents ON documents
    USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant_id')::UUID);

CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation_chunks ON document_chunks
    USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant_id')::UUID);

CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation_users ON users
    USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant_id')::UUID);

CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation_conversations ON conversations
    USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant_id')::UUID);

CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation_messages ON messages
    USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant_id')::UUID);

CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation_audit ON audit_logs
    USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant_id')::UUID);
```

<!-- RESOLVED (Worker 3): Added tenant_id directly to messages table as recommended
     in the previous NOTE. This enables direct RLS without subquery joins,
     which is critical for performance at scale. Also added idx_messages_tenant index. -->

### 2.2 S3 Bucket Structure

```
s3://docai-saas-documents/
  └── tenants/
      └── {tenant_id}/
          └── documents/
              └── {document_id}/
                  ├── original/          # Original uploaded file
                  │   └── filename.pdf
                  └── processed/         # Extracted artifacts
                      └── text.json
```

S3 bucket policy enforces tenant isolation via IAM + presigned URLs only.

### 2.3 Data Model Notes & Considerations

**Scale Estimates for pgvector:**
- 10,000 documents per tenant × ~50 chunks per document = ~500,000 chunks per tenant
- 100 tenants × 500,000 = ~50 million total chunks
- Each vector (1536 dims, float32) ≈ 6KB → ~300GB vector data at full scale
- **NOTE**: At this scale, consider partitioning `document_chunks` by `tenant_id` (range or list partitioning) to keep HNSW index sizes manageable. A single HNSW index over 50M vectors will be slow; partitioned indexes per tenant or tenant group will perform much better.

### 2.4 Partitioning Strategy for document_chunks

At full scale (50M vectors), a single HNSW index becomes impractical. Use PostgreSQL declarative partitioning:

```sql
-- Replace the document_chunks table with a partitioned version at scale
-- Phase 1 (MVP): Use single table (sufficient for early tenants)
-- Phase 2 (full product): Migrate to list partitioning by tenant_id

-- Example partitioned table structure:
CREATE TABLE document_chunks (
    id UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    tenant_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES tenants(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    document_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    chunk_index INT NOT NULL,
    content TEXT NOT NULL,
    token_count INT,
    embedding vector(1536),
    metadata JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    PRIMARY KEY (id, tenant_id)
) PARTITION BY HASH (tenant_id);

-- Create 16 hash partitions (each handles ~6 tenants, ~3M vectors)
CREATE TABLE document_chunks_p0 PARTITION OF document_chunks
    FOR VALUES WITH (MODULUS 16, REMAINDER 0);
CREATE TABLE document_chunks_p1 PARTITION OF document_chunks
    FOR VALUES WITH (MODULUS 16, REMAINDER 1);
-- ... repeat for p2 through p15

-- Each partition gets its own HNSW index (~3M vectors each, very manageable)
CREATE INDEX idx_chunks_embedding_p0 ON document_chunks_p0
    USING hnsw (embedding vector_cosine_ops) WITH (m = 16, ef_construction = 64);
-- ... repeat per partition
```

**Migration path**: Start with a single table for MVP. Add partitioning via `pg_partman` or a manual migration when approaching 5M+ chunks. The application code doesn't change — PostgreSQL routes queries to the correct partition automatically.

---

## 3. API Design

### 3.1 Authentication & Tenant Context

Every request carries:
- **JWT token** (from Cognito) → identifies user
- **Tenant context** → derived from user's `tenant_id` in DB (not from client)

Middleware flow:
1. Validate JWT → extract `cognito_sub`
2. Look up user → get `tenant_id`, `role`
3. Set PostgreSQL session variable: `SET app.current_tenant_id = '{tenant_id}'`
4. RLS automatically filters all queries

### 3.2 API Endpoints

```
BASE: /api/v1

# Auth
POST   /auth/signup                  # Create tenant + admin user
POST   /auth/login                   # Cognito login
POST   /auth/refresh                 # Refresh token
POST   /auth/forgot-password         # Password reset

# Tenant Management (admin only)
GET    /tenant                       # Get current tenant info
PUT    /tenant                       # Update tenant settings
GET    /tenant/usage                 # Usage stats

# User Management (admin only)
GET    /users                        # List users in tenant
POST   /users/invite                 # Invite user (sends email)
PUT    /users/{id}/role              # Change role
DELETE /users/{id}                   # Deactivate user

# Documents (editor, admin)
POST   /documents/upload             # Upload document (multipart)
GET    /documents                    # List documents (paginated, filterable)
GET    /documents/{id}               # Get document details + status
GET    /documents/{id}/text          # Get extracted text
GET    /documents/{id}/summary       # Get/generate summary
DELETE /documents/{id}               # Delete document (admin/owner)
POST   /documents/bulk-upload        # Upload multiple documents

# Q&A / RAG (all authenticated users)
POST   /
